title: chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. gracile
intro: Chrysocephalum apiculatum subsp. gracile, also known as the yellow buttons plant, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. The leaves of the yellow buttons plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken to harness its medicinal properties.
